LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FISHER DEVELOPMENT, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGATIONS AND CLAIM AGAINST IT IN THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFFS CALVIN MCPETERS AND TRACY BOWERS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (D302)
DECLARATION OF OTIS L. JONES ESQ. IN SUPPORT OF NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 62435970) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MC PETERS, CALVIN BOWERS, TRACY
MINI MINUTES FOR SEP-24-2018 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 17177046) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MC PETERS, CALVIN BOWERS, TRACY AS TO DEFENDANT PAGE, RICHARD FISHER DEVELOPMENT INC. DOES 1 THROUGH 20 INCLUSIVE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FISHER DEVELOPMENT INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT - DEFENDANT FISHER DEVELOPMENT, INC.'S DEMURRER TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGED IN PLAINTIFFS CALVIN MCPETERS AND TRACY BOWERS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TWENTY DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT FILE THEIR FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT BEFORE FISHER DEVELOPMENTS FILED THIS DEMURRER. HOWEVER, THE COURT OVERLOOKS THIS SHORTCOMING AS PLAINTIFFS SERVED THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ON FISHER DEVELOPMENT AND BOTH SIDES FILED PAPERS ADDRESSING THE MERITS OF DEMURRER. IF THEY HAVE NOT DONE SO ALREADY, PLAINTIFFS ARE DIRECTED TO FILE THEIR FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT AS SOON AS REASONABLY POSSIBLE. PLAINTIFFS SEEK TO HOLD FISHER LIABLE BASED ON RESPONDEAT SUPERIOR, YET PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE NO FACTS THAT DEFENDANT RICHARD PAGE WAS ACTING WITHIN THE SCOPE OF HIS EMPLOYMENT AT THE TIME OF THE INCIDENT GIVING RISE TO THIS LAWSUIT. IF THEY CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH, PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO REMEDY THIS DEFICIENCY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS CALVIN MC PETERS AND TRACY BOWERS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE A THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO REFILING THE MOTION IN ACCORDANCE WITH CRC 3.1324. BECAUSE PLAINTIFFS DID NOT FILE A PROPOSED THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT AND IT IS NOT CLEAR WHAT CHANGES THEY WISH TO MAKE TO THEIR SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE MOTION MUST BE DENIED. AS GUIDANCE FOR THE PARTIES GOING FORWARD, BUT WITHOUT PREJUDGING A PROPOSED PLEADING THAT THE COURT HAS NOT YET SEEN IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFFS MAY HAVE GOOD CAUSE TO ALLEGE A NEGLIGENT RETENTION CLAIM AGAINST DEFENDANT FISHER DEVELOPMENT INC. AND IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFFS STILL LACK SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE A VIABLE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES AGAINST FISHER. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE; HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-26-2018 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiffs Calvin Mc Peters and Tracy Bowers' motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is denied without prejudice to refiling the motion in accordance with CRC 3.1324. Because plaintiffs did not file a proposed third amended complaint and it is not clear what changes they wish to make to their second amended complaint, the motion must be denied. As guidance for the parties going forward, but without prejudging a proposed pleading that the court has not yet seen it appears that plaintiffs may have good cause to allege a negligent retention claim against defendant Fisher Development Inc. and it appears that plaintiffs still lack sufficient facts to allege a viable request for punitive damages against Fisher.
Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge; Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Second Amended Complaint

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-03-2018 Continued To Dec-03-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-01-2017 Set For Trial On Jul-02-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-15-2017 Continued To Nov-15-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FISHER DEVELOPMENT, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the punitive damages allegations and claim against it in the second amended complaint filed by plaintiffs Calvin McPeters and Tracy Bowers is GRANTED without leave to amend. The SAC lacks any facts, nor does it appear that plaintiffs are now aware of any facts, that support punitive damages liability against Fisher per the last sentence of Civil Code 3294(b). However, if plaintiffs subsequently learn facts which in good faith they believe support punitive damages liability against Fisher per that subsection, they may file a motion for leave to amend to allege punitive damages liability against Fisher. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (D302)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-17-2017 Continued To Oct-04-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT FISHER DEVELOPMENT INC. DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant Fisher Development, Inc.'s demurrer to all causes of action alleged in plaintiffs Calvin McPeters and Tracy Bowers' first amended complaint is sustained with twenty days leave to amend. Plaintiffs did not file their first amended complaint before Fisher Developments filed this demurrer. However, the court overlooks this shortcoming as plaintiffs served the first amended complaint on Fisher Development and both sides filed papers addressing the merits of demurrer. If they have not done so already, plaintiffs are directed to file their first amended complaint as soon as reasonably possible. Plaintiffs seek to hold Fisher liable based on respondeat superior, yet plaintiffs allege no facts that defendant Richard Page was acting within the scope of his employment at the time of the incident giving rise to this lawsuit. If they can do so in good faith, plaintiffs are given leave to remedy this deficiency. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 =302/hk
